_comment: Consumed by tools/security/secret-scan.py and the 'secret-scan' job in
Each allow[] entry suppresses a KNOWN, reviewed match (a test fixture or a documented
false positive). EVERY entry MUST carry path, scope, reason, owner, and expiry
(YYYY-MM-DD). 'path' is an fnmatch glob relative to the repo root; 'rules' is a list of
detector ids (or ['*'] for any). An EXPIRED entry stops suppressing — the match
re-surfaces and fails the scan — so waivers cannot rot. A real leaked secret must be

allow.reason: Intentional non-functional fake-credential fixture that proves the secret detectors fire on every run; contains no real secret.
allow.reviewCriteria: On expiry, re-confirm the fixture is still fake and still needed; remove the entry if the in-repo scanner is retired.
